hero.headline: Everyday Goods. Better Built.
hero.subheadline: A polished static storefront backed by native WooCommerce checkout, products, accounts, and admin tools.

config.title: Built for the messy middle of real WooCommerce

items.headline: WP stays in charge
items.description: Checkout, accounts, orders, and gateway hooks remain native.

items.headline: Static frontend
items.description: SvelteKit builds to files that Apache can serve on shared hosting.

items.headline: Admin controlled
items.description: Hero, modules, cards, scripts, and access modes are edited in wp-admin.

config.title: Launch with content that already looks like a store

items.heading: Hero sections, sliders, galleries, FAQs, and CTA blocks are all admin-driven.
items.description: The demo uses the same module pipeline a real store uses, so screenshots reflect actual runtime configuration.

items.heading: Products, cart, checkout, my-account, and order received stay connected to WooCommerce.
items.description: The SPA handles browsing and cart UX while native WooCommerce handles the parts plugins expect.

items.q: Does this require Node.js in production?
items.a: No. The SPA builds to static files and WordPress remains the runtime backend.

items.q: Can I still use WooCommerce plugins?
items.a: Yes for checkout, accounts, order hooks, gateways, taxes, shipping, and admin-side jobs. SPA-visible widgets may need custom integration.
Most brand, module, script, checkout, and access settings live under the WCHS menu in wp-admin.
